code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of ``Triple`` objects into a fluent, grammatically correct sentence.
    All triples sharing the same subject are combined into a single clause;
    different subjects generate separate sentences.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Detect if any runway length is expressed in feet – use this to choose
    # the appropriate unit for generic elevation predicates.
    feet_units = any("Feet" in t.predicate for t in triples)

    # Mapping of predicates to human‑readable phrase generators.
    # For elevationAboveTheSeaLevel we decide the unit based on the
    # presence of a feet‑based runway predicate.
    phrase_map = {
        # Airport‑specific
        "cityServed": lambda o: f"serves the city of {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": lambda o: f"is at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": lambda o: f"is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": lambda o: f"is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": lambda o: f"has a runway length of {o}",
        "runwayName": lambda o: f"has a runway named {o}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has runway surface {o}",
        "country": lambda o: f"is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda o: f"is part of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": lambda o: f"has a first runway length of {o} feet",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a first runway surface of {o}",
        # Runway predicates - handle generically to support any number
        # These will be handled with pattern matching below
        "2ndRunwayLengthFeet": None,
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": None,
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": None,
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": None,
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": None,
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": None,
        "5thRunwayNumber": None,
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": None,
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": None,
        "1stRunwayNumber": None,
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": lambda o: f"has ICAO code {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": lambda o: f"has location identifier {o}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": lambda o: f"has IATA code {o}",
        "nativeName": lambda o: f"is also known as {o}",
        # Geopolitical
        "leaderParty": lambda o: f"has a leader from the {o} party",
        "capital": lambda o: f"has {o} as its capital",
        "language": lambda o: f"speaks {o.replace(' language','')}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda o: f"official language is {o}",
        "leader": lambda o: f"is led by {o}",
        "mayor": lambda o: f"has mayor {o}",
        "owner": lambda o: f"is owned by {o}",
        "largestCity": lambda o: f"has {o} as its largest city",
        "countySeat": lambda o: f"has county seat {o}",
        "administrativeArrondissement": lambda o: f"is in the administrative arrondissement {o}",
        "city": lambda o: f"is located in {o}",
        "jurisdiction": lambda o: f"has jurisdiction over {o}",
        "demonym": lambda o: f"people are called {o}",
        # Military / organization
        "aircraftHelicopter": lambda o: f"operates helicopter {o}",
        "transportAircraft": lambda o: f"operates transport aircraft {o}",
        "aircraftFighter": lambda o: f"operates fighter {o}",
        "attackAircraft": lambda o: f"operates attack aircraft {o}",
        "battle": lambda o: f"was involved in the {o}",
        "hubAirport": lambda o: f"has hub airport at {o}",
        "regionServed": lambda o: f"serves the region of {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda o: f"has the leader title of {o}",
        "headquarter": lambda o: f"has headquarters at {o}",
        "currency": lambda o: f"uses currency {o}",
        # Taxonomy
        "class": lambda o: f"is classified as {o}",
        "division": lambda o: f"belongs to division {o}",
        "order": lambda o: f"belongs to order {o}",
        # Miscellaneous
        "foundingYear": lambda o: f"was founded in {o}",
        "foundedBy": lambda o: f"was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": lambda o: f"has postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": lambda o: f"has area code {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": lambda o: f"is in ceremonial county {o}",
        "chief": lambda o: f"is led by chief {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject and combine predicates into concise clauses
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_phrases: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)

    for t in triples:
        generator = phrase_map.get(t.predicate)
        # Handle runway predicates generically if not in map
        if generator is None:
            if t.predicate.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):
                rn = t.predicate.split("Runway")[0].lower()
                phrase = f"has a {rn} runway length of {t.object} feet"
            elif t.predicate.endswith("RunwayLengthMetre"):
                rn = t.predicate.split("Runway")[0].lower()
                phrase = f"has a {rn} runway length of {t.object} metres"
            elif t.predicate.endswith("RunwaySurfaceType"):
                rn = t.predicate.split("Runway")[0].lower()
                phrase = f"has {rn} runway surface {t.object}"
            elif t.predicate.endswith("RunwayNumber"):
                rn = t.predicate.split("Runway")[0].lower()
                phrase = f"has {rn} runway number {t.object}"
            else:
                phrase = f"{t.predicate} {t.object}"
        else:
            phrase = generator(t.object)
        subject_phrases[t.subject].append(phrase)

    # Build a single complex sentence that combines all subject clauses
    # Group triples by subject
    subject_triples: dict[str, list[Triple]] = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Build phrases for each subject
    subject_clauses: list[str] = []
    for subj, subj_triples in subject_triples.items():
        phrases = []
        for t in subj_triples:
            generator = phrase_map.get(t.predicate)
            phrase = generator(t.object) if generator else f"{t.predicate} {t.object}"
            phrases.append(phrase)

        if len(phrases) == 1:
            subject_clauses.append(f"{subj} {phrases[0]}")
        else:
            # Combine phrases with commas and 'and' for this subject
            subject_clauses.append(f"{subj} " + ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1])

    # Join subject clauses with proper connectors to form one complex sentence
    if len(subject_clauses) == 1:
        sentence = subject_clauses[0] + "."
    elif len(subject_clauses) == 2:
        sentence = subject_clauses[0] + ", and " + subject_clauses[1] + "."
    else:
        # Create a more complex sentence with relative clauses
        sentence = subject_clauses[0]
        for i, clause in enumerate(subject_clauses[1:], 1):
            # Extract the subject from the clause to create relative reference
            clause_subject = clause.split()[0]
            if i == len(subject_clauses) - 1:
                sentence += ", and " + clause
            else:
                sentence += "; " + clause
        sentence += "."

    # Ensure the first character is capitalised
    sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
